pi_items.source: Task specification - verified as valid Bridge Command scenario path format

pi_items.verified_value: 48 knots is consistent with Beaufort Force 10 threshold, but actual 1979 Fastnet conditions reached 60-70 knots (Force 11). Non-standard Bridge Command parameter (not in official docs).
pi_items.source: Web search: 1979 Fastnet historical wind data (60 knots measured, up to 70 knots in gusts, Force 11 at 56-63 knots). Verified via anchor_dragging_detection verifier comments that WindSpeed is non-standard extension.
Web search: 1979 Fastnet Race storm had southwesterly winds (225 degrees). Initial storm conditions matched this direction before 90-degree shift to northwesterly.
Range check: 200-250 degrees tolerance for SW winds - reasonable but not independently verified

pi_items.verified_value: 0.8 nm is reasonable approximation. Actual conditions: fog reduced visibility to 50-60 yards (~0.04 nm) initially, then 'almost impossible to see anything' during peak storm.
pi_items.source: Web search: 1979 Fastnet visibility conditions - fog at 50-60 yards minimum visibility, then severely reduced during storm. 0.8 nm represents restrictive maritime conditions.
Bridge Command documentation: Weather parameter ranges 0-12 (worst). Value 3.0 represents rough seas, appropriate for Force 10/11 storm simulation.
Task specification - 303 yachts participated in 1979 Fastnet Race. 5-vessel requirement is intentional simplification for simulation.
Nautical calculation: Running before wind in SW gale (225 degrees) requires heading northeast (~045 degrees). Mathematically correct for downwind sailing.
Reasonable tolerance for simulation, not independently verified
Task description: Rescue vessel heads 'INTO the weather (South-West)' - consistent with heading into SW wind/sea. Real rescue vessels would head into waves for stability.
Reasonable tolerance for simulation, not independently verified

privileged_info_summary: The 1979 Fastnet Race disaster occurred during August 13-14, 1979 (NOT August 15 as stated in task description). Historical conditions confirmed: Southwest winds (225°) at Force 10-11 (56-70 knots, exceeding claimed 48 knot minimum), visibility reduced by fog to 50-60 yards with further degradation during peak storm. The task uses a non-standard 'WindSpeed' parameter (not in official Bridge Command documentation) but verifier explicitly accepts this extension. 303 yachts participated; 5-vessel simulation is intentional simplification. Task creates synthetic scenario files; no real data downloaded.
